content.full_text:
June 21, 1976


Jerry E. Callister, Attorney
Griswold, Callister & Hendricks
552 West 20th Street
Merced, California 95340

Dear Mr. Callister:

     The Attorney General's office has forwarded to us your inquiry
of June 8, 1976, regarding contributions by corporations.

     Under state law, corporations may contribute to candidates for
state and local office. The only prohibition in the Political Reform
Act regarding contributors to campaigns appears in Government Code
Section 86202 which provides that a lobbyist may not make a contribution or act as an agent or intermediary in the making of any contribution or arrange for the making of any contribution by himself or
by any other person to a state candidate, a committee supporting a
state candidate or an elected state officer.

     The Fair Political Practices Commission is responsible for the
administration and implementation of the Political Reform Act, which
is a disclosure law affecting campaign financing, lobbying and conflicts of interest. If you have further questions concerning these
subjects, please contact our staff.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Nan Hambleton
                                        Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

     bcc: Jeanne P.

LAW OFFICES OF

HUGH H. GRISWOLD                          GRISWOLD, CALLISTER & HENDRICKS                    AREA CODE 209
JERRY E.CALLISTER                         652 WEST 16TH STREET                               TELEPHONE 722-8125
BRUCE J.HENDRICKS                         MERCED, CALIFORNIA 95340


                                          June 8, 1976


Office of the Attorney General
State of California
500 Capital Mall
Sacramento, California  95800

Dear Sir:

     I would very much appreciate your giving me
information regarding contributions by corporations.
We realize it is a Federal law that corporations
cannot make contributions to Federal candidates, but
is there any State law which would prohibit corpo-
rations from making contributions to State and local
candidates?

     Thank you.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          JERRY E. CALLISTER
JEC/pn

EVELLE J. YOUNGER
ATTORNEY GENERAL

STATE OF CALIFORNIA

OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL

Department of Justice
595 CAPITOL MALL, SUITE 550
SACRAMENTO 95814

(916) 322-3360

Fair Political Practices Commission
925 "L" Street, Suite 532
Sacramento, CA 95814

June 17, 1976

Re: LETTER RECEIVED BY PUBLIC INQUIRY UNIT

The enclosed letter concerns a matter which does not come
under the jurisdiction of the Attorney General's Office. We are,
therefore, referring it to you for reply.

The correspondent has been informed of our action.

Very truly yours,

EVELLE J. YOUNGER
Attorney General

JERRY LITTMAN
Chief, Information Services

JL:BS:ki
Enclosure
